Chicken Quesadillas

  • chicken
  • easy
  • mexican
  1. Whisk 2 Tbsp vegetable oil, 2 crushed garlic cloves, 1 tsp oregano, 1 Tbsp cumin, 1 tsp cayenne, 1 tsp paprika, 1 tsp white sugar, juice of 1 lime, 1 Tbsp olive oil, 1 tsp kosher salt, and 1 tsp freshly ground pepper.
  2. Cut 3 chicken breasts in half horizontally. Pound them flat as you can.1
  3. Massage chicken breast in the marinade to coat evenly.
  4. Cover and refrigerate at least 30 minutes.
  5. Preheat a skillet over high heat.
  6. Cook marinated chicken on both sides until nicely golden brown and cooked through.2
  7. Remove from skillet, let cool slightly, then slice.
  8. Top a small Sonora-style flour tortilla3 with sliced chicken, Monterey Jack cheese, and sharp cheddar cheese. Cover with another tortilla and press flat as you can.
  9. Heat vegetable oil in a skillet over medium heat.
  10. Cook quesadillas till golden brown on each side.
  11. Serve with guac, salsa, and sour cream.

Notes

Original recipe from Babish.

Footnotes

  1. Helps them cook evenly. ↩

  2. Chicken very smoky, though maybe because I was using a steel skillet. Try nonstick or oven next time. If oven, could try broiling at the end for crisp? ↩

  3. Sonora-style crisp up fast. ↩
